problems.title: Set Matrix Zeroes
problems.description:
Given an `m x n` integer matrix, if any cell contains the value 0, then the entire row and column containing that cell must be set to 0. You should perform this update in-place.

Try to do it without using extra space proportional to the matrix size.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains two integers m and n. Next m lines each contain n space-separated integers representing the matrix.
problems.outputFormat: Print the modified matrix as m lines of n space-separated integers.

examples.explanation: The cell at (1,1) is 0, so row 1 and column 1 are set to 0.
Cells (0,0) and (0,3) are 0, so rows 0 and columns 0, 3 become 0.

problems.title: Pascal's Triangle
problems.description:
Given an integer `numRows`, generate the first `numRows` of Pascal's triangle.

In Pascal's triangle, each number is the sum of the two numbers directly above it.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer numRows.
problems.outputFormat: Print numRows lines. Line i contains the i-th row of Pascal's triangle, space-separated.

problems.title: Next Permutation
problems.description:
Given an array of integers `nums`, rearrange the elements to form the lexicographically next greater permutation. If no such permutation exists, rearrange it to the lowest possible order.

The modification must be done in-place using only constant extra memory.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Second line contains n space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the next permutation as n space-separated integers.

examples.explanation: [3,2,1] is the largest; wraps to smallest.

problems.title: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's Algorithm)
problems.description:
Given an integer array `nums`, find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return that sum.

A subarray is a contiguous part of an array.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Second line contains n space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print a single integer — the maximum subarray sum.

examples.explanation: Subarray [4,-1,2,1] has the largest sum of 6.

problems.title: Sort Colors (Dutch National Flag)
problems.description:
Given an array `nums` containing only 0, 1, and 2, sort it in-place so all 0s come first, then 1s, then 2s.

Solve without a library sort in a single pass with O(1) extra space.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Second line contains n space-separated integers (each 0, 1, or 2).
problems.outputFormat: Print the sorted array as n space-separated integers.

problems.title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
problems.description:
You are given an array `prices` where `prices[i]` is the stock price on the i-th day. Maximize profit by choosing one day to buy and a future day to sell.

Return maximum profit. If no profit is possible, return 0.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Second line contains n space-separated integers (prices).
problems.outputFormat: Print a single integer — the maximum profit.

examples.explanation: Prices only fall; no profitable transaction.

problems.title: Rotate Matrix by 90 Degrees
problems.description: You are given an `n x n` 2D matrix. Rotate the image 90 degrees clockwise in-place. Do not allocate another 2D matrix.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Next n lines each contain n space-separated integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the rotated matrix as n lines of n space-separated integers.

problems.title: Merge Overlapping Intervals
problems.description: Given an array of intervals `[start, end]`, merge all overlapping intervals and return the non-overlapping intervals covering all input intervals.
problems.inputFormat: First line contains integer n. Next n lines each contain two integers: start and end.
problems.outputFormat: Print merged intervals one per line as 'start end', sorted by start.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Arrays In-Place
problems.description: Given two sorted arrays `nums1` (size m+n with n placeholders) and `nums2` (size n), merge nums2 into nums1 in sorted order in-place.
problems.inputFormat: First line: m and n. Second line: m+n integers (nums1). Third line: n integers (nums2).
problems.outputFormat: Print the merged sorted array as m+n space-separated integers.

problems.title: Find the Duplicate Number
problems.description: Given n+1 integers in range [1,n] with exactly one repeated number, find it without modifying the array and using O(1) extra space.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n+1 (size). Second line: n+1 integers.

problems.title: Repeat and Missing Number
problems.description: Given an array of size n containing integers from 1 to n where one is repeated and one is missing, find both.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print repeated and missing numbers, space-separated.

problems.title: Count Inversions in Array
problems.description:
Count the number of inversions in an array. An inversion is a pair (i,j) where i < j but arr[i] > arr[j].

Use merge sort for O(n log n).
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.

problems.title: Search in a 2D Matrix
problems.description: Given an m×n matrix (rows sorted, first element of each row > last of previous), find if a target exists. Aim for O(log(m*n)).
problems.inputFormat: First line: m, n, target. Next m lines: n integers each.

problems.title: Pow(x, n)
problems.description:
Implement pow(x, n) — x raised to the power n — in O(log n) using binary exponentiation. Handle negative exponents.

Print with 5 decimal places.
problems.inputFormat: A single line: real number x and integer n.

problems.title: Majority Element (more than n/2 times)
problems.description:
Return the majority element — the element appearing more than ⌊n/2⌋ times. It always exists.

Solve in O(n) time and O(1) space using Boyer-Moore Voting.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.

problems.title: Majority Element II (more than n/3 times)
problems.description:
Find all elements appearing more than ⌊n/3⌋ times. Return them in ascending order.

There can be at most two such elements. Solve in O(n) time and O(1) space.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print qualifying elements in ascending order, space-separated. Empty line if none.

problems.title: Grid Unique Paths
problems.description:
A robot on an m×n grid starts at (0,0) and needs to reach (m-1,n-1). It can only move right or down.

Return the number of unique paths.
problems.inputFormat: A single line: two integers m and n.
problems.outputFormat: Print the number of unique paths.

problems.title: Reverse Pairs
problems.description: Return the number of reverse pairs: pairs (i,j) where i < j and nums[i] > 2 * nums[j].
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the count of reverse pairs.

problems.title: 4 Sum
problems.description: Return all unique quadruplets [a,b,c,d] from the array that sum to target. Each quadruplet in non-decreasing order, output sorted lexicographically.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n and target. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Each quadruplet on a separate line. Empty output if none.

problems.title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
problems.description: Given an unsorted array, return the length of the longest sequence of consecutive integers. Solve in O(n).
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print the length of the longest consecutive sequence.

problems.title: Largest Subarray with Zero Sum
problems.description: Find the length of the longest contiguous subarray whose elements sum to zero. Return 0 if none.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.

problems.title: Count Subarrays with Given XOR
problems.description: Count the number of contiguous subarrays whose XOR equals B.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n and B. Second line: n integers.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.description: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
problems.inputFormat: A single line containing string s.

problems.title: Reverse a Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse it and return the new head.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n space-separated node values.
problems.outputFormat: Print the reversed list values, space-separated.

problems.title: Middle of the Linked List
problems.description: Return the middle node. If two middles exist (even length), return the second one.
problems.inputFormat: First line: n. Second line: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print from middle node onwards, space-separated.

problems.title: Merge Two Sorted Linked Lists
problems.description: Given two sorted linked lists, merge them into one sorted list.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: m, then m integers (list1). Line 2: n, then n integers (list2). Use 0 for empty.
problems.outputFormat: Print merged sorted values, space-separated. Empty output if both empty.

problems.title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
problems.description: Remove the n-th node from the end of the list and return its head. Try to do it in one pass.
problems.inputFormat: First line: size and n. Second line: list values.
problems.outputFormat: Print result list. Empty output if list becomes empty.

problems.title: Add Two Numbers (Linked List)
problems.description: Two linked lists store non-negative integers in reverse order digit-by-digit. Add them and return the sum as a linked list in reverse order.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: m, then m digits (list1). Line 2: n, then n digits (list2).

problems.title: Delete Node Without Head Pointer
problems.description: Delete a node from a linked list without access to the head (not the tail). Copy the next node's value and unlink it.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and idx (0-indexed, not tail). Line 2: n values.

problems.title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
problems.description: Find the node where two linked lists intersect. Described by exclusive parts a, b and common tail c. Print value of intersecting node, or -1.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: a b c. Lines 2-4: values for a-only, b-only, common (empty lines if 0).

problems.title: Detect Cycle in Linked List
problems.description: Determine if a linked list has a cycle. Input gives list values and pos (index tail's next points to, or -1).
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and pos. Line 2: n values.

problems.title: Reverse Linked List in Groups of K
problems.description: Reverse the nodes of a linked list k at a time. If remaining nodes < k, leave them as-is.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and k. Line 2: n values.

examples.explanation: Reverse first triple; remaining two stay.

problems.title: Check if Linked List is Palindrome
problems.description: Return true if the linked list values form a palindrome.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n values.

problems.title: Find Starting Point of Cycle
problems.description: Find the node where the cycle begins. Print value, or -1 if no cycle.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and pos. Line 2: n values.

problems.title: Flatten a Multilevel Linked List
problems.description: Flatten k sorted sub-lists (chains) into a single sorted list. Input gives k chains.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: k. Next k lines: chain values terminated by -1.

problems.title: Rotate Linked List
problems.description: Rotate a linked list to the right by k places.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and k. Line 2: n values.

problems.title: 3 Sum
problems.description: Return all unique triplets [a,b,c] summing to 0. Each triplet sorted ascending; output lexicographic.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Each triplet on a new line. Empty if none.

problems.title: Trapping Rain Water
problems.description: Given heights of bars, compute total units of water trapped after raining.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n heights.

problems.title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
problems.description: Remove duplicates in-place from a sorted array. Return k (count of unique elements); first k elements are unique.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n sorted integers.
problems.outputFormat: First line: k. Second line: k unique values.

problems.title: Max Consecutive Ones
problems.description: Return the maximum number of consecutive 1s in a binary array.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n values (0 or 1).

problems.title: N Meetings in One Room
problems.description: Find maximum meetings in one room (no overlapping). A meeting (s1,e1) and (s2,e2) overlap if s2 <= e1.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n start times. Line 3: n end times.

problems.title: Minimum Number of Platforms
problems.description: Find minimum railway platforms needed so no train waits. Times in HHMM integer format.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n arrivals. Line 3: n departures.

problems.title: Job Sequencing Problem
problems.description: Schedule jobs (each takes 1 unit) to maximize profit. A job earns profit only if done by its deadline. Print count and total profit.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Next n lines: 'id deadline profit'.

problems.title: Fractional Knapsack
problems.description: Given n items with value and weight, and capacity W, find maximum value using fractional items. Print with 2 decimal places.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and W. Next n lines: 'value weight'.
problems.outputFormat: Print max value with 2 decimal places.

examples.explanation: All of item1,2 and 2/3 of item3.

problems.title: Minimum Number of Coins
problems.description: Given amount V (INR), find minimum coins/notes from [1,2,5,10,20,50,100,200,500,2000]. Print count and denominations.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer V.
problems.outputFormat: Line 1: count. Line 2: denominations in non-increasing order.

problems.title: Activity Selection
problems.description: Select maximum activities from start/finish pairs. Activities are non-overlapping if next starts strictly after previous finishes.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n start times. Line 3: n finish times.

problems.title: Subset Sums
problems.description: Return the sums of all 2^n subsets in non-decreasing order.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Print all 2^n subset sums in non-decreasing order, space-separated.

problems.title: Combination Sum
problems.description: Return all unique combinations from distinct candidates that sum to target. Same number may be used unlimited times.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and target. Line 2: n distinct candidates.
problems.outputFormat: Each combination on a new line, space-separated.

problems.title: Combination Sum II
problems.description: Return unique combinations from candidates (may have duplicates) summing to target. Each number used at most once.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and target. Line 2: n integers.
problems.outputFormat: Each combination on a new line.

problems.title: Palindrome Partitioning
problems.description: Partition string s such that every substring is a palindrome. Return all such partitionings.
problems.inputFormat: A single line: string s.
problems.outputFormat: Each partitioning on a new line, parts separated by space.

testCases.expectedOutput:
a a a
a aa
aa a
aaa

problems.title: K-th Permutation Sequence
problems.description: Return the k-th permutation sequence of [1..n] in lexicographic order (1-indexed).
problems.inputFormat: A single line: n and k.
problems.outputFormat: Print the k-th permutation as a digit string.

problems.title: Print All Permutations
problems.description: Return all permutations of distinct integers in lexicographic order.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n distinct integers.
problems.outputFormat: Each permutation on a new line, space-separated.

problems.title: N Queens
problems.description: Count distinct solutions to place n queens on an n×n board so no two attack each other.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer n.

problems.title: Sudoku Solver
problems.description: Given a partially filled 9×9 Sudoku board ('.' for empty), solve it. Exactly one solution exists.
problems.inputFormat: 9 lines of 9 characters (digits 1-9 or '.').
problems.outputFormat: Print solved 9×9 board, 9 lines of 9 characters.
constraints: Valid Sudoku with exactly one solution

problems.title: M Coloring Problem
problems.description: Given a graph and m colors, determine if vertices can be colored such that no adjacent vertices share a color.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: V E m. Next E lines: edges as 'u v' (0-indexed).

problems.title: Rat in a Maze
problems.description: Find all paths in a binary n×n maze from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1) using D/L/R/U moves. Each cell visited once per path. Output lexicographically sorted.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Next n lines: n space-separated 0/1 values.
problems.outputFormat: Each path string on a new line. Empty if no path.

problems.title: Nth Root of an Integer
problems.description: Find integer n-th root of m (x where x^n = m exactly). Print -1 if no integer root.
problems.inputFormat: A single line: n and m.

problems.title: Single Element in a Sorted Array
problems.description: In a sorted array where every element appears twice except one, find the single element in O(log n).
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n (odd). Line 2: n sorted integers.

problems.title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
problems.description: Given a sorted array rotated at unknown pivot, find target index in O(log n), or -1.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and target. Line 2: n integers.

problems.title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problems.description: Find median of two sorted arrays in O(log(min(m,n))). Print with 5 decimal places.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: m and n. Line 2: m sorted integers. Line 3: n sorted integers.

problems.title: Allocate Minimum Number of Pages
problems.description: Allocate n books to m students (consecutive books per student) minimizing maximum pages assigned. Print -1 if m > n.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and m. Line 2: n page counts.

problems.title: Aggressive Cows
problems.description: Place c cows in n stalls to maximize minimum distance between any two cows.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and c. Line 2: n stall positions.

problems.title: K-th Largest Element in an Array
problems.description: Return the k-th largest element (in sorted order, not distinct).
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and k. Line 2: n integers.

problems.title: Merge K Sorted Arrays
problems.description: Given k sorted arrays, merge into one sorted array.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: k. Next k lines: size then sorted integers.

problems.title: Top K Frequent Elements
problems.description: Return k most frequent elements. Break frequency ties by smaller value first.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and k. Line 2: n integers.

problems.title: Check for Balanced Parentheses
problems.description: Given a string of '()[]{}'  characters, determine if it is valid (properly nested and matched).
problems.inputFormat: A single string s.

problems.title: Next Greater Element
problems.description: For each element, find the next greater element to its right. Print -1 if none.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n integers.

examples.explanation: Next greater on right for each.

problems.title: LRU Cache
problems.description: Design LRU cache with capacity C. '1 key value' = put, '2 key' = get (print or -1). Evict least recently used on overflow.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: C and q. Next q lines: queries.
problems.outputFormat: For each '2' query print value or -1.

problems.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problems.description: Find the area of the largest rectangle in a histogram of bar heights with width 1.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n heights.

problems.title: Sliding Window Maximum
problems.description: Return the maximum value in every window of size k.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n and k. Line 2: n integers.

problems.title: Min Stack
problems.description: Stack supporting push/pop/top/getMin in O(1). '1 x'=push, '2'=pop, '3'=top, '4'=getMin. Print -1 if empty.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: q. Next q lines: queries.

problems.title: Rotting Oranges (BFS)
problems.description: Grid: 0=empty, 1=fresh, 2=rotten. Each minute, rotten spreads 4-directionally. Find minimum minutes to rot all, or -1 if impossible.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: m and n. Next m lines: n integers.

problems.title: Stock Span Problem
problems.description: For each day's price, the span is the count of consecutive days up to today where price <= today's. Compute all spans.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n prices.

problems.title: Reverse Words in a String
problems.description: Reverse word order in a string. Trim leading/trailing spaces; separate words by single space.
problems.inputFormat: A single line s.

problems.title: Longest Palindromic Substring
problems.description: Return the longest palindromic substring. If multiple have same length, return first.
problems.inputFormat: A single line s.

problems.title: Roman to Integer
problems.description: Convert Roman numeral to integer. Symbols: I=1,V=5,X=10,L=50,C=100,D=500,M=1000. Subtractive forms: IV=4,IX=9,XL=40,XC=90,CD=400,CM=900.
problems.inputFormat: A single Roman numeral string.

problems.title: String to Integer (atoi)
problems.description: Implement atoi: skip whitespace, optional sign, read digits until non-digit. Clamp to 32-bit int range.
problems.inputFormat: A single line s.

problems.title: Longest Common Prefix
problems.description: Find the longest common prefix among an array of strings. Print empty if none.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: n. Line 2: n space-separated strings.

problems.title: KMP / strStr
problems.description: Find first occurrence index of needle in haystack using KMP, or -1 if not found.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: haystack. Line 2: needle.

problems.title: Valid Anagram
problems.description: Return true if t is an anagram of s (same characters with same multiplicities).
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: s. Line 2: t.

problems.title: Count and Say
problems.description: Return the n-th term of the count-and-say sequence. countAndSay(1)='1'; for n>1, run-length encode previous term.
problems.inputFormat: A single integer n.

problems.title: Compare Version Numbers
problems.description: Compare version strings v1 and v2. Return -1/0/1. Leading zeros ignored; missing revisions = 0.
problems.inputFormat: Line 1: v1. Line 2: v2.
